steps.title: Use the opening claim
steps.content: The narrator says people are 'creatures of the sun' who love light and life, which is why they crowd into towns and cities. The rest of the passage develops that idea by contrasting daylight enjoyment of nature with nighttime fear and loneliness.

steps.title: Match the purpose
steps.content: Choice A wrongly places isolation in crowded areas. Choice C says the passage cautions readers to value rural beauty, and Choice D reverses the preference by saying people prefer being alone in the country. The passage explains traits that draw people to cities. The answer is B.
